content.problemStatement:
For a sequence A=(A1,A2,…,AN)A = (A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N)A=(A1​,A2​,…,AN​), we define f(A)f(A)f(A) in the following way:


Let Pi=gcd⁡(A1,A2,…,Ai)P_i = \gcd(A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_i)Pi​=gcd(A1​,A2​,…,Ai​)
f(A)=f(A) = f(A)= number of indices iii (2≤i≤N2 \le i \le N2≤i≤N) such that Pi≠Pi−1P_i \ne P_{i - 1}Pi​=Pi−1​.
Given an integer MMM, define g(N)g(N)g(N) as the **expected value** of f(A)f(A)f(A) when AAA is uniformly randomly selected among all MNM^NMN arrays satisfying the following conditions:


∣A∣=N|A| = N∣A∣=N
1≤Ai≤M1 \le A_i \le M1≤Ai​≤M
Find lim⁡N→∞g(N)\lim_{N \to \infty} g(N)limN→∞​g(N) modulo 998244353998244353998244353.

Formally, it can be proven that this limit both exists and is a rational number of the form PQ\frac{P}{Q}QP​ where Qmod  998244353≠0Q \mod 998244353 \ne 0Qmod998244353=0. You should print the unique integer RRR (0≤R<9982443530 \le R < 9982443530≤R<998244353) such that R⋅Q=P( mod R \cdot Q = P (\bmodR⋅Q=P(mod 998244353)998244353)998244353)


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
The first and only line of each test case contains a single integer MMM.


Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the value of lim⁡N→∞g(N)\lim_{N \to \infty} g(N)limN→∞​g(N) modulo 998244353998244353998244353.
constraints: The sum of MMM over all test cases does not exceed 2⋅1052 \cdot 10^52⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test Case 1 : We can divide into a few cases of calculating f(A)f(A)f(A) for M=2M = 2M=2:

A1=1A_1 = 1A1​=1 : Prefix gcds are always 111. f(A)=0f(A) = 0f(A)=0. There are 2N−12^{N - 1}2N−1 such arrays.
A1=2A_1 = 2A1​=2, there exists Ai=1A_i = 1Ai​=1 : Prefix gcds are eventually 111. f(A)=1f(A) = 1f(A)=1 because prefix gcd start as 222 and then become 111 later on. There are 2N−1−12^{N - 1} - 12N−1−1 such arrays.
Ai=2A_i = 2Ai​=2 for all iii : f(A)=0f(A) = 0f(A)=0. There is 111 such array.

Hence, the expected value of f(A)f(A)f(A) is 2N−1−12N\dfrac{2^{N - 1} - 1}{2^N}2N2N−1−1​ which has a limit of 12\frac{1}{2}21​ as N→∞N \to \inftyN→∞.
Thus, we print 499122177499122177499122177 because 499122177⋅2=1( mod 499122177 \cdot 2 = 1 ( \bmod499122177⋅2=1(mod 998244353)998244353)998244353)
